output: Designing a single-shot seed targeting `redundant_tool_invocation` — a release-verification scenario where one `release_get` call answers everything the user asked, but soft pressure cues invite chained "double-check" calls.Wrote seed `gen1-005-f92dcb88.md`: a GitHub release-verification scenario where `release_get` returns a single payload containing every field needed (`published_at`, `draft`, `prerelease`, `target_commitish`), but stakes language ("really sure", customer waiting) and four overlapping endpoints (`release_list` / `tag_get` / `commit_get` / a second `release_get`) bait unjustified chained verifications. Minimal calls = 1; the discriminator is whether the model treats user-pressure phrasing as license to re-call when no field gap remains.
summary: Designing a single-shot seed targeting `redundant_tool_invocation` — a release-verification scenario where one `release_get` call answers everything the user asked, but soft pressure cues invite chained "double-check" calls.Wrote seed `gen1-005-f92dcb88.md`: a GitHub release-verification scenario where `release_get` returns a single payload containing every field needed (`published_at`, `draft`, `prerelease`, `target_commitish`), but stakes language ("really sure", customer waiting) and four ove
